perturbation.original_text: All information disclosed under this Agreement is deemed Confidential Information whether or not it is so marked. It is the intent of the Parties to mark information as confidential and/or proprietary prior to release to the Receiving Party. However, such markings may be overlooked and or disclosed verbally or visually and shall not diminish the value of its confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: Only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure is considered Confidential Information. While the Parties intend to mark information as confidential, unmarked information is not protected under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original provision stating that all information disclosed is deemed confidential, regardless of marking. The updated text now makes the marking a strict requirement, creating a contradiction about which information is protected. This contradicts the original text that says even unmarked information can be deemed confidential, this change causes an uncertainty on which information is to be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Marking . Only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure is considered Confidential Information. While the Parties intend to mark information as confidential, unmarked information is not protected under this agreement. It is the intent of the Parties to mark information as confidential and/or proprietary prior to release to the Receiving Party. However, such markings may be overlooked and or disclosed verbally or visually and shall not diminish the value of its confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' is considered Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original text which states that all information disclosed is deemed Confidential Information, whether or not it is so marked. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the two sections provide conflicting guidelines on what constitutes confidential information under the agreement. The contradiction is triggered in the contradicted text which contains both the original text and changed text, leading to uncertainty about which version is correct.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party for the return of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information, at the Receiving Party's discretion, may be retained indefinitely for archival purposes, unless the Disclosing Party makes a written request for its return. Upon such request, only the original documents, and not copies, need to be returned.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the 'Return of Confidential Information' clause. Originally, all confidential information had to be returned promptly upon request. The updated version allows indefinite retention and specifies only originals, not copies, need to be returned. This undermines the original clause's intent of ensuring the Disclosing Party regains control of its information. This change creates an uncertainty on when the Disclosing party gets the confidential information back.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information . Confidential Information, at the Receiving Party's discretion, may be retained indefinitely for archival purposes, unless the Disclosing Party makes a written request for its return. Upon such request, only the original documents, and not copies, need to be returned. All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party for the return of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party'. The changed text contradicts this by stating that 'Confidential Information, at the Receiving Party's discretion, may be retained indefinitely for archival purposes' and 'only the original documents, and not copies, need to be returned.' This directly conflicts with the initial requirement to return all confidential information, including copies, upon request, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information may be used for any purpose the Receiving Party deems necessary or beneficial, including but not limited to internal research, product development, and competitive analysis, irrespective of whether it directly benefits the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified clause now states that the Receiving Party can use the Confidential Information for *any* purpose they deem beneficial, regardless of whether it benefits the disclosing party or if it aligns with the original intent. This completely undermines the limitations set in the original clause and introduces an in-text contradiction that the Receiving party can do as it deems fit with the Confidential Information regardless if it benefits the other party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Limitation on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . Confidential Information may be used for any purpose the Receiving Party deems necessary or beneficial, including but not limited to internal research, product development, and competitive analysis, irrespective of whether it directly benefits the Disclosing Party. Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships. (a) Confidential Information shall not be copied or reproduced by the Receiving Party, except for such copies as may be reasonably required for accomplishment of the purposes stated herein; (b) Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to employees, agents, and other parties of the Receiving Party who have a “need to know” in connection with the purposes stated herein; and (c) This Agreement shall not restrict the disclosure or use of information that: (i) was in the public domain at the time of disclosure or thereafter enters the public domain through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (ii) was, at the time of the receipt by the Receiving Party, otherwise known to the Receiving Party without restrictions as to the use or disclosure; (iii) becomes known to the Receiving Party from a source other than the disclosing party without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; or (iv) is developed independently by the Receiving Party and without reliance upon the Confidential Information disclosed herein. 7. Trading Limitations . The Parties’ will comply with any applicable United States securities laws.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party can use the Confidential Information for 'any' purpose they deem beneficial, irrespective of whether it benefits the Disclosing Party. The contradicted text, specifically: 'Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships' directly conflicts with the changed text and introduces a contradiction within the document. The contradicted text also contains the same conflicting implications as the changed text. Therefore it is a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Except as provided herein, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California, USA in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect for the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any provision herein to the contrary, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively through litigation in the courts of England.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates arbitration in San Diego. The altered clause mandates litigation in England. The contradictory terms create uncertainty about the dispute resolution process, this uncertainty is due to the explicit different location and method for dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration . Notwithstanding any provision herein to the contrary, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively through litigation in the courts of England. Except as provided herein, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California, USA in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect for the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, the Parties may undertake a reasonable amount of discovery. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgement may be entered on the arbitrator’s decision in any court having jurisdiction. (b) Equitable Remedies . The Parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the Parties agree that if in the event of a breach of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specify performance of any such provision of the Agreement. The Parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the Parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly changes the dispute resolution method from arbitration in San Diego to litigation in England. The contradicted text includes both the original arbitration clause and the new litigation clause in England, creating a direct conflict. The change removes the arbitration clause and replaces it with a different dispute resolution method and location. This directly contradicts the original clause that stipulates arbitration in San Diego, creating a structural flaw based on placement and leading to uncertainty about the dispute resolution process.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof, superseding all prior contemporaneous communications, agreements and understandings between the Parties with respect to the disclosure and protection of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement does *not* constitute the entire understanding between the Parties; it is supplemented by oral agreements and implied understandings, which take precedence over any conflicting written terms herein.
perturbation.explanation: This directly contradicts the 'Entire Understanding' clause. The original text asserts that the written agreement is the complete and final understanding, superseding all prior agreements. The updated version states that oral agreements and implied understandings are *also* part of the agreement and take precedence, undermining the written contract's authority. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the validity and scope of the written terms. this also completely undercuts the original statement that there are no outside agreements that take precedence over the written agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Understanding . This Agreement does *not* constitute the entire understanding between the Parties; it is supplemented by oral agreements and implied understandings, which take precedence over any conflicting written terms herein. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof, superseding all prior contemporaneous communications, agreements and understandings between the Parties with respect to the disclosure and protection of Confidential Information. The rights and obligations of the Parties shall be limited to those expressly set forth herein. nStor Corporation, Inc. Xyratex Technology Limited /s/ Steve Aleshire /s/ Matt Cornell Authorized Signature Authorized Signature Matt Cornell Steve Aleshire Print Name Print Name Executive VP – Storage Systems COO Title Title September 3, 2004 9/3/04 Date Date 3
perturbation.justification: The original text's 'Entire Understanding' clause is directly contradicted by the changed text, which states that the agreement is supplemented by oral agreements and implied understandings that take precedence. The contradicted text includes both versions of the 'Entire Understanding' clause, highlighting the direct conflict. The change undermines the written contract's authority, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it violates the original assertion that there are no outside agreements that take precedence, fitting the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
